title: AD Taylor-tower P9: differentiable control flow through loops, branches, recursion, closures, named-let, map/fold
goal: Per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md Frontier P9. Make tower AD robust through ordinary Scheme control flow: if/cond/case, named-let loops, recursion, self/mutual TCO, closures, higher-order calls, map, and fold. Preserve perturbation safety by carrying the tower epoch tag in flags[16..31] across branches, loop phis, tail calls, closure environments, and list combinators; foreign-tag towers are constants in the current derivative context. Branch derivatives follow the primal-selected piece; at kinks the default is an explicit differentiability error unless both arms agree through K or an opt-in subgradient policy is declared.
acceptance: control-flow AD oracle differentiates data-dependent loops and branches to d=8 vs analytic references
named-let, recursion, self/mutual TCO, nested derivative-in-branch, closure, map, and fold cases PASS
kink policy enforced: error unless branch derivatives agree through K or explicit subgradient policy is selected
scripts/run_control_flow_tests.sh, scripts/run_recursion_depth.sh, scripts/run_tco_tests.sh, and scripts/run_ad_depth.sh PASS
campaign_payoff: Turns the Taylor tower from a straight-line numeric-kernel differentiator into language-level AD over real Scheme programs.
